Aizawa.citation: Aizawa, Yoji, and Tatsuya Uezu (1982). Topological Aspects in Chaos and in 2 k-Period Doubling Cascade. Progress of Theoretical Physics 67.3 (1982): 982-985. See also Langford (1984). Numerical studies of torus bifurcations. Numerical Methods for Bifurcation Problems. Birkhauser, Basel, 1984. 285-295.
Aizawa.description: A torus-like attractor related to the forced Lorenz system.

AnishchenkoAstakhov.citation: Anishchenko, et al. Nonlinear dynamics of chaotic and stochastic systems: tutorial and modern developments. 2007.

Arneodo.citation: Arneodo, A., Coullet, P. & Tresser, C. Occurence of strange attractors in three-dimensional Volterra equations. Physics Letters A 79, 259--263 (1980)
Arneodo.description: A modified Lotka-Volterra ecosystem, also known as the ACT attractor.

ArnoldBeltramiChildress.citation: V. I. Arnold, Journal of Applied Mathematics and Mechanics 30, 223 (1966).
ArnoldBeltramiChildress.description: An exact solution of Euler's equation for inviscid flow in 3D. Often referred to as the ABC flow.

AtmosphericRegime.citation: Tuwankotta (2006). Chaos in a coupled oscillators system with widely spaced frequencies and energy-preserving non-linearity
AtmosphericRegime.description: A model of regime transition in atmospheric flows. Contains widely-separated timescales due a quadratic term, which itself arises from a conservation law.

BeerRNN.citation: Beer, R. D. (1995). On the dynamics of small continuous-time recurrent neural networks. Adapt. Behav., 3(4), 4692013509.
BeerRNN.description: A two-neuron minimal model nervous system.

BelousovZhabotinsky.citation: Gyorgyi and Field (1992). A three-variable model of deterministic chaos in the Belousov-Zhabotinsky reaction. Nature.
BelousovZhabotinsky.description: A reduced-order model of the BZ reaction that exhibits period doubling. The bifurcation parameter for controlling the onset of chaos is kf. The system undergoes regular cycling when kf=3e-4, and chaotic oscillations when kf=3.5e-4

BickleyJet.citation: Hadjighasem, Karrasch, Teramoto, Haller (2016). A Spectral Clustering Approach to Lagrangian Vortex Detection Phys Rev E.
BickleyJet.description: A zonal jet passing between two counter rotating vortices. A steady Hamiltonian with a time-dependent perturbation.

Blasius.description: A chaotic food web composed of interacting predators,, consumers, and vegetation.

Bouali.citation: Bouali (1999). Feedback loop in extended Van der Pols equation applied to an economic model of cycles. International Journal of Bifurkation and Chaos, Vol. 9, No. 4
Bouali.description: Economic cycles with fluctuating demand. Related to the DequanLi attractor

Bouali2.citation: Bouali (1999). Feedback loop in extended Van der Pols equation applied to an economic model of cycles. International Journal of Bifurkation and Chaos, Vol. 9, No. 4

BurkeShaw.description: A scroll-like attractor with unique symmetry along the z axis.

CaTwoPlus.citation: Houart, Dupont, Goldbeter. Bull Math Biol 1999.

CaTwoPlusQuasiperiodic.citation: Houart, Dupont, Goldbeter. Bull Math Biol 1999.
CaTwoPlusQuasiperiodic.description: Intracellular calcium ion oscillations with quasiperiodic parameter values. The original paper also includes parameters for a limit cycle

CellularNeuralNetwork.citation: Arena, Caponetto, Fortuna, and Porto., Int J Bifurcat Chaos. 1998:  1527-1539.

Chen.citation: Chen (1997). Proc. First Int. Conf. Control of Oscillations and Chaos.
Chen.description: A system based on feedback anti-control in engineering.

ChenLee.citation: Chen HK, Lee CI (2004). Anti-control of chaos in rigid body motion. Chaos, Solitons & Fractals
ChenLee.description: A rigid body with feedback anti-control.

Chua.citation: Chua, L. O. (1969) Introduction to Nonlinear Network Theory, McGraw-Hill, New York.
Chua.description: An electronic circuit with a diode providing negative resistance.

CoevolvingPredatorPrey.description: A system of predator-prey equations with co-evolving prey.

Colpitts.citation: Kennedy (2007). IEEE Trans Circuits & Systems. 1994: 771-774. Li, Zhou, Yang. Chaos, Solitons, & Fractals.
Colpitts.description: An electrical circuit used as a signal generator.

Coullet.citation: Arneodo, A., Coullet, P. & Tresser, C. Occurence of strange attractors in three-dimensional volterra equations. Physics Letters A 79, 259--263 (1980)
Coullet.description: A variant of the Arneodo attractor

Dadras.citation: S Dadras, HR Momeni (2009). A novel three-dimensional autonomous chaotic system generating two, three and four-scroll attractors. Physics Letters A.
Dadras.description: An electronic circuit capable of producing multiple attractors under bifurcations.

DequanLi.description: Related to the Three Scroll unified attractor TSUCS-2

DoublePendulum.citation: See, for example: Marion (2013). Classical dynamics of particles and systems.
DoublePendulum.description: Two coupled rigid pendula without damping.

ExcitableCell.citation: Teresa Chay. Chaos In A Three-variable Model Of An Excitable Cell. Physica D 1985
ExcitableCell.description: A reduced-order variant of the Hodgkin-Huxley model. The parameter gkc controls the onset of chaos.

Finance.citation: Guoliang Cai, Juanjuan Huang. International Journal of Nonlinear Science, Vol. 3 (2007)
Finance.description: Stock fluctuations under varying investment demand and interest rates

GenesioTesi.citation: Genesio, Tesi (1992). Harmonic balance methods for the analysis of chaotic dynamics in nonlinear systems. Automatica 28.3 (1992): 531-548.
GenesioTesi.description: A nonlinear control system with feedback.

GlycolyticOscillation.citation: Decroly, O.; Goldbeter, An amplified sensitivity arising from covalent modification in biological systems. A. Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 1982, 79,6917–6921.
GlycolyticOscillation.description: A model of feedback in glycolytic reactions. Based on the Monod–Wyman–Changeux model.

GuckenheimerHolmes.citation: Guckenheimer, John, and Philip Holmes (1983).  Nonlinear oscillations, dynamical systems, and bifurcations of vector fields. Vol. 42. Springer Science & Business Media.

Hadley.citation: G. Hadley (1735). On the cause of the general trade winds. Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society, vol. 34, pp. 58--62.

Halvorsen.citation: Sprott, Julien C (2010). Elegant chaos: algebraically simple chaotic flows. World Scientific, 2010.
Halvorsen.description: An algebraically-simple chaotic system with quadratic nonlinearity

HenonHeiles.citation: Henon, M.; Heiles, C. (1964). The applicability of the third integral of motion: Some numerical experiments. The Astronomical Journal. 69: 73.
HenonHeiles.description: A star's motion around the galactic center.

HindmarshRose.description: A neuron model exhibiting spiking and bursting.

HyperBao.citation: Bao, Liu  (2008). A hyperchaotic attractor coined from the chaotic Lu system. Chin. Physics Letters, Vol. 25, pp 2396-2399.

HyperCai.citation: Guoliang, Huang (2007). A New Finance Chaotic Attractor. International Journal of Nonlinear Science.
HyperCai.description: A hyperchaotic variant of the Finance system.

HyperJha.citation: Jürgen Meier (2003). Presentation of Attractors with Cinema.

HyperLorenz.citation: Jürgen Meier (2003). Presentation of Attractors with Cinema.
HyperLorenz.description: A hyperchaotic variant of the Lorenz attractor.

HyperLu.citation: Chen, A., Lu, J., Lü, J., & Yu, S. (2006). Generating hyperchaotic Lü attractor via state feedback control. Physica A: Statistical Mechanics and its Applications, 364, 103-110.
HyperLu.description: A hyperchaotic variant of the Lu attractor.

HyperPang.citation: Pang, S., & Liu, Y. (2011). A new hyperchaotic system from the Lü system and its control. Journal of Computational and Applied Mathematics, 235(8), 2775-2789.

HyperQi.citation: G. Qi, M. A. van Wyk, B. J. van Wyk, and G. Chen, (2008). On a new hyperchaotic system. Physics Letters A.
HyperQi.description: A hyperchaotic variant of the Qi system.

HyperRossler.citation: Rossler, O. E. (1979). An equation for hyperchaos.
HyperRossler.description: A hyperchaotic variant of the Rossler system.

HyperWang.citation: Wang, Z., Sun, Y., van Wyk, B. J., Qi, G. & van Wyk, M. A (2009). A 3-D four-wing attractor and its analysis. Brazilian J. Phys. 39.
HyperWang.description: A hyperchaotic variant of the Wang system.

HyperYan.citation: Jürgen Meier (2003). Presentation of Attractors with Cinema.

HyperYangChen.citation: Jürgen Meier (2003). Presentation of Attractors with Cinema.

IsothermalChemical.citation: Petrov, Scott, Showalter. Mixed-mode oscillations in chemical systems. J Chem Phys 1992
IsothermalChemical.description: An isothermal chemical system with mixed-mode oscillations

ItikBanksTumor.citation: Itik, Banks. Int J Bifurcat Chaos 2010
ItikBanksTumor.description: A model of cancer cell populations.

JerkCircuit.citation: Sprott (2011). A new chaotic jerk circuit. IEEE Circ. Sys. 2011.
JerkCircuit.description: An electronic circuit with nonlinearity provided by a diode.

KawczynskiStrizhak.citation: P. E. Strizhak and A. L. Kawczynski, J. Phys. Chem. 99, 10830 (1995).
KawczynskiStrizhak.description: A chemical oscillator model describing mixed-modes in the BZ equations.

Laser.citation: Abooee, Yaghini-Bonabi, Jahed-Motlagh (2013). Analysis and circuitry realization of a novel three-dimensional chaotic system. Comm Nonline Sci 2013

LiuChen.citation: Liu, Chen. Int J Bifurcat Chaos. 2004: 1395-1403.

Lorenz.citation: Lorenz, Edward N (1963). Deterministic nonperiodic flow. Journal of the atmospheric sciences 20.2 (1963): 130-141.
Lorenz.description: A minimal weather model based on atmospheric convection.

Lorenz84.citation: E. Lorenz (1984). Irregularity: a fundamental property of the atmosphere. Tellus A, vol. 36, no. 2, pp. 98--110, 1984.
Lorenz84.description: Atmospheric circulation analogous to Hadley convection.

Lorenz96.citation: Lorenz, Edward (1996). Predictability: A problem partly solved. Seminar on Predictability, Vol. I, ECMWF.
Lorenz96.description: A climate model containing fluid-like advective nonlinearities, and multiscale coupling of fast and slow variables.

LorenzBounded.description: The Lorenz attractor in the presence of a confining potential.

LuChen.citation: Lu, Chen. Int J Bifurcat Chaos. 2002: 659-661.
LuChen.description: A system that switches shapes between the Lorenz and Chen attractors as the parameter c is varied from 12.7 to 28.5. Also called the Chen Celikovsky attractor

LuChenCheng.citation: Lu, Chen, Cheng. Int J Bifurcat Chaos. 2004: 1507--1537.
LuChenCheng.description: A four scroll attractor that reduces to Lorenz-like dynamics under bifurcations.

MooreSpiegel.citation: Moore, Spiegel. A Thermally Excited Nonlinear Oscillator, Ap. J., 143, 871 (1966)

MultiChua.citation: Mufcstak E. Yalcin, Johan A. K. Suykens, Joos Vandewalle. Cellular Neural Networks, Multi-Scroll Chaos And Synchronization.

NewtonLiepnik.citation: Leipnik, R. B., and T. A. Newton (1981). Double strange attractors in rigid body motion with linear feedback control. Physics Letters A 86.2 (1981): 63-67.
NewtonLiepnik.description: Euler's equations for a rigid body, augmented with a linear feedback term.

NoseHoover.citation: Nose, S (1985). A unified formulation of the constant temperature molecular-dynamics methods. Journal of Chemical Physics (1984). Hoover, William G. Canonical dynamics: Equilibrium phase-space distributions. Phys. Rev. A. Hoover, William G., Anthony JC Ladd, and Bill Moran (1982). High-strain-rate plastic flow studied via nonequilibrium molecular dynamics. Physical Review Letters.
NoseHoover.description: Fixed temperature molecular dynamics for a strained flow under a reduced order model.

NuclearQuadrupole.citation: Baran V. and Raduta A. A. (1998), International Journal of Modern Physics E
NuclearQuadrupole.description: A quadrupole boson Hamiltonian that produces chaos via a resonance overlap mechanism

PanXuZhou.citation: Zhou, Wuneng, et al. On dynamics analysis of a new chaotic attractor. Physics Letters A 372.36 (2008): 5773-5777..
PanXuZhou.description: A named attractor related to the DequanLi attractor

PehlivanWei.citation: Pehlivan, Ihsan, and Wei Zhouchao (2012). Analysis, nonlinear control, and chaos generator circuit of another strange chaotic system.
PehlivanWei.description: A system with quadratic nonlinearity, which undergoes Hopf bifurcations of codimension 1 and 2.

Qi.citation: G. Qi, M. A. van Wyk, B. J. van Wyk, and G. Chen, (2008). On a new hyperchaotic system. Physics Letters A.
Qi.description: A hyperchaotic system with a wide power spectrum.

QiChen.citation: Qi et al. Chaos, Solitons & Fractals 2008.
QiChen.description: A double-wing chaotic attractor that arises from two bistable attractors. The system has quadratic nonlinearity.

RabinovichFabrikant.citation: Rabinovich, Mikhail I.; Fabrikant, A. L. (1979). Stochastic Self-Modulation of Waves in Nonequilibrium Media. Sov. Phys. JETP.
RabinovichFabrikant.description: A reduced-order model of propagating waves in monequilibrium media.

RayleighBenard.citation: Yanagita, Kaneko (1995). Rayleigh-Bénard convection patterns, chaos, spatiotemporal chaos and turbulence. Physica D: Nonlinear Phenomena 82.3 (1995): 288-313.
RayleighBenard.description: A reduced-order model of a convective cell.

RikitakeDynamo.citation: Rikitake, T., Oscillations of a system of disk dynamos, Mathematical Proceedings of the Cambridge Philosophical Society, 1958.
RikitakeDynamo.description: Electric current and magnetic field of two coupled disk dynamos.

Rossler.citation: Rossler, O. E. (1976), An Equation for Continuous Chaos, Physics Letters, 57A (5): 3972013398,
Rossler.description: Spiral-type chaos in a simple oscillator model.

Rucklidge.citation: Rucklidge, A.M. (1992). Chaos in models of double convection. Journal of Fluid Mechanics.
Rucklidge.description: Two-dimensional convection in a horizontal layer of Boussinesq fluid with lateral constraints.

Sakarya.citation: Li, Chunbiao, et al (2015). A novel four-wing strange attractor born in bistability. IEICE Electronics Express 12.4.
Sakarya.description: An attractor that arises due to merging of two disjoint bistable attractors.

SaltonSea.citation: Upadhyay, Bairagi, Kundu, Chattopadhyay (2007).Chaos in eco-epidemiological problem of the Salton Sea and its possible control. Applied mathematics and computation.
SaltonSea.description: An eco-epidemiological model of bird and fish abundances in the Salton sea of southern California.

SanUmSrisuchinwong.description: A two-scroll attractor arising from dynamical equations with piecewise nonlinearities. Equivalent to the Wimol-Banlue system

ShimizuMorioka.description: A system that bifurcates from a symmetric limit cycle to an asymmetric one, similar to the Lorenz attractor.

SprottA.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottA.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottB.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottB.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottC.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottC.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottD.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottD.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottE.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottE.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottF.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottF.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottG.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottG.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottH.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottH.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottI.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottI.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottJ.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottJ.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottJerk.citation: Sprott, J. C. Simplest dissipative chaotic flow. Physics Letters A (1997).
SprottJerk.description: An algebraidally simple flow depending on a third time derivative. Numerical integration of this attractor is extremely sensitive to the initial conditions

SprottK.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottK.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottL.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottL.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottM.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottM.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottMore.citation: Sprott, J. C. (2020). Do We Need More Chaos Examples? Chaos Theory and Applications 2(2),1-3, 2020.
SprottMore.description: A multifractal system with a nearly 3D attractor

SprottN.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottN.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottO.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottO.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottP.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottP.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottQ.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottQ.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottR.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottR.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottS.citation: Sprott (1994). Some simple chaotic flows. Physical Review E (1994).
SprottS.description: A member of the Sprott family of algebraically-simple chaotic sytems.

SprottTorus.description: A multiattractor system that goes to a torus or a complex attractor depending on the initial conditions.

SwingingAtwood.citation: Tufillaro, Nicholas B.; Abbott, Tyler A.; Griffiths, David J. (1984). Swinging Atwood's Machine. American Journal of Physics. 52 (10): 895--903.
SwingingAtwood.description: A mechanical system consisting of two swinging weights connected by ropes and pulleys. This is only chaotic when m2 is sufficiently larger than m1, and there are nonzero initial momenta

Thomas.citation: Thomas, Rene (1999). Deterministic chaos seen in terms of feedback circuits: Analysis, synthesis, labyrinth chaos. Int. J. Bifurc. Chaos.
Thomas.description: A cyclically-symmetric attractor correspondng to a frictionally-damped particle traversing a 3D lattice of forces

ThomasLabyrinth.citation: Thomas, Rene. Deterministic chaos seen in terms of feedback circuits: Analysis, synthesis, labyrinth chaos. Int. J. Bifurc. Chaos. (1999).
ThomasLabyrinth.description: A system in which trajectories seemingly undergo Brownian motion on an infinite lattice, but remain bounded when the parameter a is less than one.

Torus.citation: See, for example, Strogatz (1994). Nonlinear Dynamics and Chaos.
Torus.description: A minimal quasiperiodic flow on a torus. All lyapunov exponents and related quantities are zero

Tsucs2.citation: Pan, Zhou, Li (2013). Synchronization of Three-Scroll Unified Chaotic System (TSUCS) and its hyper-chaotic system using active pinning control
Tsucs2.description: A named attractor related to the DequanLi attractor

VallisElNino.citation: Vallis GK. Conceptual models of El Nio and the southern oscillation. J Geophys Res 1988
VallisElNino.description: Atmospheric temperature fluctuations with annual forcing.

WangSun.citation: Wang, Z., Sun, Y., van Wyk, B. J., Qi, G. & van Wyk, M. A (2009). A 3-D four-wing attractor and its analysis. Brazilian J. Phys. 39.

WindmiReduced.citation: Smith, Thiffeault, Horton. J Geophys Res. 2000. Horton, Weigel, Sprott. Chaos 2001.
WindmiReduced.description: Energy transfer into the ionosphere and magnetosphere by the solar wind. The parameter vsw controls the onset of chaos.

YuWang.citation: Yu, Wang (2012). A novel three dimension autonomous chaotic system with a quadratic exponential nonlinear term. Eng Techn & Applied Science Research.
YuWang.description: A temperature-compensation circuit with an operational amplifier

YuWang2.citation: Yu, Wang (2012). A novel three dimension autonomous chaotic system with a quadratic exponential nonlinear term. Eng Techn & Applied Science Research.
YuWang2.description: An alternative temperature-compensation circuit with an operational amplifier

ZhouChen.citation: Zhou, Chen (2004). A simple smooth chaotic system with a 3-layer attractor. Int J Bifurcat Chaos, 2004
